mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to enhance their security posture in a cloud-native environment. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and the potential performance impact during vulnerability scanning.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator from it.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify they are running.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to define how the Trivy Operator should scan resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/alpine
    tag: latest
EOF

```

> Creates a default TrivyConfiguration for scanning the Alpine image.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/alpine
    tag: latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the TrivyConfiguration
steps.description:
This step checks that the TrivyConfiguration resource is created and ready.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the TrivyConfiguration resources to verify the creation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related configurations and data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfiguration-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current TrivyConfiguration to a YAML file.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfiguration-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivyconfiguration.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io "default" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Recreate the TrivyConfiguration resource using the provided YAML definition.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matching manifest for linux/amd64 in the manifest list entries'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image tag may not be available for your architecture.

**Fix:**
Verify the image tag and ensure it supports your architecture.

troubleshooting.title: Trivy scans are not triggering
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyOperator may not be configured correctly to watch for resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the TrivyConfiguration is set up correctly and that the operator has the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image scanning configurations and improved performance for large clusters.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' to confirm.
codeSnippets:

apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/alpine
    tag: latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
